name: Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
shortDescription: Ujjain's central pilgrimage and one of the twelve revered Jyotirlingas.
longDescription: Plan Mahakal darshan as the anchor of the Ujjain visit. Booking, entry, dress and reporting rules should always be verified through official temple sources before travel.
idealDuration: Keep 2–4 hours as a flexible planning window; verify for your travel date.

name: Harsiddhi Mata Temple
shortDescription: A historic Shakti temple close to the Mahakal and Ram Ghat circuit.
longDescription: Its iconic lamp towers and old-city setting make it a natural companion to Mahakal. Keep the visit unhurried and confirm access conditions during festivals.
idealDuration: Approximately 45–60 minutes, subject to crowd conditions.

name: Kal Bhairav Temple
shortDescription: An ancient shrine associated with Ujjain's guardian deity and distinctive local traditions.
longDescription: Visit in the morning when possible and prepare family members for the temple's distinctive ritual setting. Queues and traffic can vary substantially.
idealDuration: Approximately 60–90 minutes; allow more time on busy days.

name: Mangalnath Temple
shortDescription: A peaceful temple associated with Mars and Ujjain's astronomical traditions.
longDescription: Often included in a wider Ujjain temple circuit. The riverside setting can suit families seeking a quieter stop between major shrines.
